content:
Goal
* Please change the following phrases:

  • The rules are => Each conditional rule is

  • computation algorithm => formula

  • It can be described as => Each rule consists of

  • After such rules will always be a final rule => After these conditional rules, a default rule is provided

  • The orders themselves will always be provided with => Each order contains

  • (last paragraph) final rule => default rule

  • (for both conditional rules and default rule) must be multiplied by the quantity => a unit cost

  • (for both conditional rules and default rule) A cost in euros => The cost in euros

* Conditional rule examples: Please modify either the first or the second example to illustrate > or >= instead of having two examples with <.

Input
* First line: Please rewrite as:

An integer [[ruleCount]] for the number of conditional rules.

* Next ruleCount lines:
  • Please replace "Rules" with "Each line is a conditional rule".

  • Please add "separated by a space" at the end.

* Next line (1):
  • Please replace "Final rule" with "Default rule".

  • Please update the variable names accordingly.

  • Please add "separated by a space" at the end.

* Next line (2): Please rewrite as:

An integer [[orderCount]] for the number of orders.

* Next orderCount lines:
  • Please change "Orders" to "Each line is an order".

  • Please add "separated by a space" at the end.

Output
* Please remove "First".

* Please add "in euros, one line per order" at the end.

Constraints
* Please change both upper bounds from 98 to 10.

* Please change the lower bound of [[orderCount]] to 1.

* Please remove ", i.e. 0 <= any number < 2^31".

Stub generator
* Please update all variable names that contain "final".

Cases
* Please replace all occurrences of "final rule" with "default rule".

* Please add one or more pairs of comprehensive cases with:
  • ruleCount = orderCount = 9
  • conditional rules on pallets, parcels and kg
  • conditional rules using all operators: <=, <, >= and >
  • conditional rules on both fixed and unit costs
  • conditional rules are arranged in a shuffled (unsorted random) order

Cover picture
* Please consider uploading a cover picture for the puzzle.

Thanks.

Thanks for the feedback! Updated relative to your feedback, except for the test cases for now.

I'm still kind of on the fence on whether I want to keep making this puzzle or not tbh, which is why the puzzle is in WIP (although I should probably have put "WIP:" in the title from the start). It's based on some work I did for a supply chain company a few years ago, and while it's good experience to have as a developer, it's not as fun as other puzzles on the site, hence the hesitation.

I'll update the test cases if I decide to push this puzzle further. Thanks again ^^

That makes sense. This problem leans more toward real-world logic than puzzle-style fun, but CG does include that kind of problem too, so it could still be a solid addition if you decide to keep going with it.

Just some small wording tweaks I noticed:

Goal
* Please change "less or equal to 3" to "less than or equal to 3".

Output
* Please remove "Computed" (more natural).

If you end up shelving the contribution, that's totally fair; but if you keep going, just minor polish and more cases are needed at this point.

Thanks.
